findings.signature: sqliteVec.load(database)
findings.notes: loadExtension() call — Turso/libSQL has no loadExtension support; sqlite-vec cannot be loaded at runtime

findings.signature: database.pragma('journal_mode = WAL')
findings.notes: WAL pragma on main database; Turso uses its own replication/WAL and may reject arbitrary pragma calls

findings.signature: database.pragma(shema.journal_mode/cache_size/mmap_size/temp_store/wal_autocheckpoint)
findings.notes: Five pragma calls on attached vector shard; Turso lacks .pragma() and ATTACH — entire shard initialization must be redesigned

findings.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_memories USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[dim])
findings.notes: sqlite-vec vec0 virtual table — Turso has no vector index support; KNN search path blocks migration entirely

findings.signature: CREATE TEMP VIEW/TRIGGER INSTEAD OF INSERT/DELETE on vec_memories
findings.notes: TEMP VIEW + INSTEAD OF triggers as compatibility shim; Turso lacks temp views and INSTEAD OF triggers

findings.signature: ATTACH DATABASE / DETACH DATABASE active_vec
findings.notes: ATTACH/DETACH for vector shard separation; Turso has no ATTACH DATABASE — shard architecture requires rework

findings.signature: close_db() → swapFn() → initialize_db(targetMainPath)
findings.notes: Atomic DB swap relies on close/reopen cycle with WAL TRUNCATE checkpoint; Turso checkpoint semantics differ

findings.signature: pragma journal_mode=WAL, busy_timeout=10000, foreign_keys=ON, cache_size, mmap_size, synchronous=NORMAL, wal_autocheckpoint, temp_store=MEMORY
findings.notes: Eight pragma calls at startup; most are unsupported in Turso — need abstraction layer or removal

findings.signature: live_db.transaction((rows) => { INSERT OR IGNORE INTO checkpoints })
findings.notes: Transaction-wrapped merge of checkpoint catalog from backup; INSERT OR IGNORE — Turso supports basic transactions but no ATTACH for the backup source

findings.signature: ATTACH legacy + shard → copy vec tables → INSERT INTO vec0 → DETACH → VACUUM
findings.notes: Full migration pipeline uses ATTACH, vec0 virtual table creation, INSERT INTO vec0, DETACH, VACUUM — every construct blocks Turso

findings.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE shard.vec_memories USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[dim]) + INSERT rowid/embedding
findings.notes: Creates vec0 virtual table in attached shard and copies rows — Turso has no vec0, no ATTACH, no loadExtension

findings.signature: database.transaction(() => { for v: migrations[v]() })
findings.notes: All 37 schema migrations wrapped in one atomic transaction; Turso supports transactions but migration DDL may hit unsupported features

findings.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
findings.notes: FTS5 virtual table for BM25 full-text search — Turso/libSQL lacks FTS5; entire FTS search path blocks migration

findings.signature: CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON memory_index → INSERT INTO memory_fts
findings.notes: FTS5 sync triggers (insert/update/delete) — dependent on FTS5 which Turso lacks; needs alternative sync mechanism

findings.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET importance_tier='deprecated' WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM (...) ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY ...))
findings.notes: Uses window functions (ROW_NUMBER OVER PARTITION BY) in migration — Turso supports window functions, but the deprecation logic couples to tier semantics

findings.signature: ALTER TABLE memory_index RENAME → CREATE TABLE → INSERT INTO → DROP TABLE (table rebuild for CHECK constraint)
findings.notes: Full table rebuild to enforce CHECK constraint; Turso may handle this differently — verify ALTER TABLE RENAME and rebuild compatibility

findings.signature: DELETE FROM active_vec.vec_memories WHERE rowid = ? (orphaned vector cleanup)
findings.notes: Deletes from vec0 virtual table in attached schema; blocked by Turso's lack of vec0 and ATTACH

findings.signature: db.transaction(() => { DELETE FROM vec_memories; INSERT INTO vec_memories(rowid, embedding) })
findings.notes: Transaction-wrapped vec0 virtual table writes (delete+insert per row) — blocks Turso migration entirely

findings.signature: new Database(shardPath) → pragma WAL → sqliteVec.load → CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec0 → writeVectors + writeVectorsToKnn → wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)
findings.notes: Full shard write path: opens separate DB, loads sqlite-vec, creates vec0, writes vectors, checkpoints WAL — every step blocks Turso

findings.signature: jobDb.transaction(() => { setActiveEmbedder() + UPDATE memory_index SET embedding_status='success' })
findings.notes: Transaction commits active-embedder pointer flip and embedding status reconciliation atomically; basic transaction — Turso compatible if table operations are
